---
name: Request a source
about: Propose a corpus, dictionary, or edition for ingestion — lead with the license.
title: "Source request: "
labels: source-request
---

<!--
Thank you for proposing a source. The single most useful thing you can
bring is license evidence: a link to the upstream grant, quoted verbatim.
A source with no verifiable open license cannot be ingested, however
desirable — so lead with the license.
-->

## Upstream

- **Name:**
- **URL** (project page and, if known, the bulk-data location — git repo, download, API):

## License, as far as known

Quote the upstream license grant **verbatim** and link to where it is
stated. If you can, name the class it falls into: `open` (CC0 / public
domain), `attribution` (CC BY / CC BY-SA), or `nc` (non-commercial or
otherwise restricted).

> (verbatim license text or "could not find a stated license")

Note: license verification happens **survey-first** — accepted proposals go
through an evidence-cited license survey (see `docs/oe-survey.md`,
`docs/slavic-survey.md`, `docs/pie-survey.md` for the pattern) before any
adapter work begins. What you provide here is the head start, not the
verdict.

## Why it fits

Which reader does it serve (see "Who this is for" in the README —
classicists, biblical scholars, Slavists, comparativists, Indologists,
papyrologists, epigraphists, Assyriologists, medievalists, linguists), or
which existing axis does it extend? What would it let you ask that the
current shelves cannot answer?

## Size and format, if known

Rough document/entry count, on-disk size, data format (TEI, CoNLL-U, CSV,
Turtle, plain text, …).
